item.title: A Most Iowa Thanksgiving. Mon Nov 24, 2025
item.description:
We had an early thanksgiving celebration in Iowa over the weekend, and it was a very Iowa weekend indeed. We tried some more of the food and gels from that care package that The Feed sent. And then we opened more packages


Packages unboxed:

The Feed - RNWY, RAW Creatine, Precision Fuel Gels

Book - Floris Gierman

Saucony Triumph 23

item.title: I didn't go to NYC Martathon. Monday Nov 3, 2025
item.description: I didn't go to New York. I had such fomo. I was obsessed with it and also trying to avoid it. The race was exciting though. packages unboxed today:TopoNaakI mentioned that I would share the allergen information for Naak, and then I think I forgot to. but here is what they sent me:"Boost Gel/Drink Mix, Ultra Energy Drink Mixes, and the purees are all free of peanuts and tree nuts or traces thereof."They also directed me to their product pages which would have more information regarding other allergens.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko

Also, we go over some Boston cutoff predictions

item.title: Live from Boston Marathon - The Fastest Game, powered by adidas
item.description: We had a chance to play our favorite livestream games, live, in Boston during Boston Marathon Weekend 2025. Adidas invited the Kofuzi Run Club to their popup space in an event called, The Fastest Game, powered by adidas. We had livestream regulars, later but lives, and friends in attendance. The audio isn’t quite as good as I would have liked, and that is on me (not the audio team that was on site - they were excellent). But I hope you can enjoy playing along with these games. 0:00 intro2:57 Name That Shoe 11:08 Who’s Time is It19:28 Triviadisclosures: thanks to Adidas for providing this space, the audio services, and the av support for Kofuzi Run Club to meet and have some laughs during Boston Marathon Weekend.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko
item.itunes:summary: We had a chance to play our favorite livestream games, live, in Boston during Boston Marathon Weekend 2025. Adidas invited the Kofuzi Run Club to their popup space in an event called, The Fastest Game, powered by adidas. We had livestream regulars, later but lives, and friends in attendance. The audio isn’t quite as good as I would have liked, and that is on me (not the audio team that was on site - they were excellent). But I hope you can enjoy playing along with these games. 0:00 intro2:57 Name That Shoe 11:08 Who’s Time is It19:28 Triviadisclosures: thanks to Adidas for providing this space, the audio services, and the av support for Kofuzi Run Club to meet and have some laughs during Boston Marathon Weekend.

item.title: Victoria Sekely - Train Smart Run Strong
item.description: In this final interview from Houston 2025, I spoke with Victoria Sekely, TrainSmartRunStrong, who I’ve been following on Instagram for what feels like years. I had the chance to bump into her while getting coffee, and I was excited to follow up here to talk about here Houston Half PB, her mantras, incorporating strength for runners, and her relationship with social media 0:00 intro 1:02 meeting in Houston 2:13 cucumber 7:26 race day shoe 8:00 mileage 11:38 not discussing paces 15:07 higher mileage and strength training 17:28 strategies for the most strength-reluctant runners 20:33 squat rack 22:44 relationship with social media 26:40 being anti-algorithm 28:33 growth is painful sometoimes Follow Victoria IG TrainSmart Run Strong https://www.instagram.com/trainsmartrunstrong/?hl=en Work with Victoria https://www.trainsmartrunstrong.com

item.title: Erika Kemp - 2nd Place Houston Marathon 2025
item.description: In this Bonus Episode, I speak with Erika Kemp, professional road runner for Brooks, after her 2nd place performance at the Houston Marathon 2025. Not only was this race an 11 minute PB, Erika was also able to lower her own record for Fastest American Born Black Runner. We talk about her race, he race days shoes, and trusting in her training. 0:00 intro 1:41 feeling strong at the end of the race 2:45 how cold was it 4:09 warming up for marathon vs 5k 5:31 start line congestion 6:24 what was the race plan with your pacer 7:10 race nutrition 9:22 maintaining composure throughout the race 10:28 deciding how to cover the moves 11:57 running the full marathon in houston for the first time 14:24 race day shoes 16:33 Erika's Shoe Rotation 19:40 when did the leader break away? 22:46 what's it like to wear the flag? 24:58 was Erika surprised 25:51 Fastest American Born Black Woman 27:15 what was different training for houston 29:01 9 day cycle 29:45 volume for this training block 30:55 believe in the training 31:38 seeing it all come together 32:33 track szn? Follow Erika on IG https://www.instagram.com/imtinyrik/reels/
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko

item.title: Morgan McDonald - Half Marathon Debut Houston 2025
item.description:
In this bonus episode, I talk with Morgan McDonald who recently ran 60:58 in his half marathon debut in Houston. We talk about his race shoes, whether he’s disappointed as a newly minted 60 minute guy, and where he sits on my list of Faster Half Marathon YouTubers


0:00 intro

1:17 race day shoes

3:13 was it cold

4:43 is he disappointed

6:52 falling off the lead pack

8:28 finding rhythm

8:43 race nutrition

9:43 australian half marathon record 

10:56 Morgan's Shoe Rotation

13:03 lightspray

14:07 half marathon mileage vs 5k mileage

15:55 why youtube

18:44 favorite kind of video to make

20:00 Fastest Half Marathon YouTube 


Follow Morgan: https://www.youtube.com/@MorganMcDonaldisaChicken https://www.instagram.com/morganmcdonald__/

hit the Play button on the bingo website to have a card generated for you. and then probably the best way to keep track of it would be to screeenshot it so you can mark off your bingo card as events of 2025 unfold.

item.title: Sara Vaughn, 1st American and 6th Overall in New York 2024
item.description:
Sara Vaughn just finished as top American in New York and 6th overall. And this was all just a few weeks after having to drop out early in Chicago. In this bonus episode, we talk about her New York Marathon, what it meant for her, the shoes she trains in, and of course, we’ll talk about her YouTube channel. 

interview date: Nov 8, 2024

0:00 intro

1:35 race shoe for New York

2:49 experience in Fast R1 and Fast R2

5:25 Puma training shoes

7:42 from Chicago to New York

9:02 why do Boulder runners love New York?

10:24 her pack in New York

11:48 was this a good result?

13:27 is it vindication or a comeback?

14:41 worst pre-race bus

15:59 Boston vs New York

17:27 off season

19:32 NIL as a parent of an athlete

21:39 Fastest Marathon YouTuber


Corrected Fastest Marathon YouTuber List

1. Molly Seidel 2:23 

2. Sara Vaughn 2:23

3. Nell Rojas 2:24

4. Phily Bowden 2:25

5. Anya Culling 2:34 


Sara Vaughn IG https://www.instagram.com/smevaughn/

Sara Vaughn Youtube https://www.youtube.com/@smevaughn


Thumbnail photo by Chase Fleming Photo https://www.chaseflemingphoto.com

item.title: Lindsay Flanagan Chicago Marathon 2024 Recap
item.description:
On today’s Bonus Episode, we have Lindsay Flanagan, who ran a marathon PB of 2:23:31 to finish as second American and ninth overall at the 2024 Chicago Marathon. We talked about how Chicago is a homecoming for her, the prototype shoes she ran in, her race/pacing plan, her nutrition, and how (even though she ran a PB) she really should have run 1 second faster. 


0:00 intro

0:50 Chicago homecoming

3:19 Race week shoes

6:22 race plan

9:40 pre-race vibes

11:35 Gabby Rooker is so nice

11:50 Rory Linkletter chatty on the long run

12:15 Centro Runs a Marathon

13:26 race nutrition plan

16:03 Lindsay’s favorite part of the course

16:44 Lindsay’s splits

17:47 wind and temps

19:35 World Championships Standard

item.title: London Marathon Recap - Coaching Call with Sage and Sandi
item.description: London Marathon didn't quite go as I had hoped, and I finally sit down with my coaches to debrief and troubleshoot. The early elevated HR definitely signaled that something was wrong. What exactly it signaled, I'm still not sure. But we go over some things that it could have been that, either alone or taken as a whole, could help explain what went wrong. 0:00 intro 2:59 headspace going in to the race 6:38 working during a taper 10:30 how I felt during the race 14:53 elevated HR 18:14 feeling cold 20:20 I'm a half tights guy 22:40 poor meal planning 27:54 too many beers? 30:59 tweaking the training plan 35:35 plan moving forward For further detail: Strava: https://www.strava.com/activities/11231514057 London Marathon Race video: https://youtu.be/flAQNO5CGJ0?si=y9Ip2z9QS_jlTDaO London Marathon Runner's Weekend: https://youtu.be/ClfMSlVzi64?si=CmNmyeds1NkBIn0F Find Sage and Sandi: Coaching at Higher Running https://higherrunning.com Sage on YouTube https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCh_w_vLvlZNBeTAP8qaWhoA Sandi on IG https://www.instagram.com/sandinypaver/

item.title: Boston Celebration Athlete Panel
item.description: Katelyn Tuohy, Cyprian Kotut, and Coach Jack Waitz join Kofuzi for a discussion about running, legacy, and of course, running shoes during the Boston Marathon weekend 2024. Kofuzi asked Katelyn whether she could ever see herself lining up for a marathon. 2:04 Marathoner Cyprian Kotut shared his secrets for managing pain during the marathon. And Coach Jack Waitz talked about some of the strangest training conditions he and his late wife and marathon legend, Grete Waitz had to endure while training in Norway. 0:00 intro 2:50 when did Katelyn fall in love with the sport 10:07 Jack Waitz describes winter running for Grete Waitz 13:17 Katelyn looks back on her time at NC State 15:45 Cyprian Kotut on his running legacy 16:51 Jack looks back on first Women's Olympic Marathon 18:01 Katelyn on NIL and the future of women's sports 21:20 Cyprian's mental prep for the marathon 22:21 will Katelyn ever race a marathon? 23:13 Cyprian's Boston Marathon race day shoe 25:00 Katelyn's shoe rotation
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko

item.title: Coaching Call - Sandi Nypaver and Sage Canaday - London Marathon 2024 Training
item.description:
YWGO, today, I’ll be sharing a recent coaching call I had with my new coaches, Sage and Sandi of Higher Running. I’ve been following each of them for years, so when it came time to start thinking about how to approach a PB attempt I’d like to make for the London Marathon, they were my first choice of coaches to work with. I’m about halfway through my training for London, so I thought this would be a good time to check in with them to talk about how things are going. We’ll talk about tough workouts, being uncomfortable, and nutrition. I hope you enjoy this conversation with Sage and Sandi.


0:00 intro

0:31 How did they write the plan

4:24 Why I Wanted a Coach

8:48 How I’m dealing with the faster shorter reps

12:35 I don’t like longer reps

15:23 A workout is just information (make clip)

17:53 Pivoting to more race-specific work

21:56 What’s the point of running between easy and marathon effort in a long run

25:38 How Many Gels

31:41 Pros get 8 bottles per race

35:03 Should I be taking in salt during a race?


Higher Running https://higherrunning.com/

Higher Running IG https://www.instagram.com/higherrunning/

Higher Running YouTube https://www.youtube.com/@HigherRunning

Sage IG https://www.instagram.com/sagecanaday/

Sage YouTube https://www.youtube.com/@Vo2maxProductions

Sandi IG https://www.instagram.com/sandinypaver/

item.title: Lisa Weightman - 4x Olympic Marathoner Chasing Her 5th Australian Olympic Team
item.description:
Lisa Weightman is a four time Olympian for Team Australia in the Marathon. Lisa recently raced the Osaka Marathon 2024, placing on the podium with a 2:24:43. In this interview, we talk about her race, the ASICS Metaspeed Prototype that she raced in, and the implications of this race on the changes of making a 5th Australian Olympic team.

We also go over her day job (she's an associate partner at IBM), her Chicago connection, and my favorite Australian TV shows.


Interview date: February 28, 2024 (although with the time zones, this was February 29, 2024 for Lisa)


0:00 intro

2:47 race recap

7:31 is Osaka hilly?

10:14 how does Osaka help Lisa get to Paris?

15:53 is Lisa a hot weather runner?

17:48 where would Lisa go to prepare for Paris?

19:26 Lisa’s Chicago Connection

22:39 does Lisa’s son like running?

24:56 will there be any more seasons of Inbestigators?

26:16 Metaspeed Sky Prototype

30:29 Lisa’s ASICS shoe rotation

31:46 carbon for workouts or races only?

32:33 Lisa’s day job

39:37 Lisa at the corporate 5k

41:32 does Lisa have an Olympic ring tattoo?

42:50 how does Lisa celebrate making an Olympic team?

44:40 what prep for Paris heat and hills?

47:23 how do you adjust nutrition training for a hot race?



Links

https://www.instagram.com/lisaweightman/

https://tempojournal.com/article/osaka-marathon-weightman-and-pashley-chase-olympic-selection/

item.title: Dakotah Lindwurm. February 15, 2024
item.description:
In this bonus episode, I speak with Dakotah Lindwurm after her recent third place finish at the US Marathon Trials in Orlando. Dakotah is sponsored by Puma, and is coached by Chris Lundstrom of Minnesota Distance Elite.


0:00 intro

1:29 race recap

3:09 does it get physical at the start

4:09 what did if feel like early in the lead pack

5:22 what do athletes hear when people cheer for them

6:10 where was coach Chris Lundstrom

6:41 what was it like on the east end of the route?

7:16 Dakotah’s bottles

8:19 lap 2

9:51 mindset at around mile 18

11:00 words of encouragement from competitors

12:10 is anyone talking during the race?

13:05 final lap

15:10 the home stretch

16:16 mill city

17:36 no little American flag?

18:33 baby on the course

20:02 big Dakotah 

20:32 speaking with Annie Frisbie

21:26 sunglasses

24:14 Puma Deviate Elite 3

item.title: Habtamu Cheney - January 20, 2024
item.description:
Habtamu Cheney just graduated from college and ran a 63:19 half marathon debut (20 seconds shy of the US Trials standard) and then followed that up with a 2:16:12 marathon debut. But he’s got an enormous amount of upside, now that he’s gotten into the Conner Mantz group chat and is being coached by Ed Eyestone. In this conversation, we talk about how he’s feeling with the US Marathon Trials in Orlando just two weeks away, what shoes he’s been training in, and what he’ll be racing in.


0:00 intro

1:08 105 miles this week

3:05 background

5:24 who’s coaching?

7:58 getting into the group chat

11:33 recovery

12:34 who’s chattiest on the group run?

13:53 is Habs worried about heat in Orlando?

14:34 heat acclimation

14:56 goals for Orlando

15:58 running partners in Orlando

16:36 Habtamu’s shoe roation

19:07 Raceday kit

19:51 Raceday nutrition

20:25 Habtamu hired a videographer for Orlando

24:39 do treadmill miles count?


Follow Habtamu’s Journey

https://www.youtube.com/@runnerhabs

https://www.instagram.com/runnerhabs/

https://www.tiktok.com/@runnerhabs

item.title: Bonus Episode! Adam Wood
item.description:
Sub Elite and unsponsored Adam Wood is living the dream, chasing after his first OTQ. He's a BYU alum and comes from a new line of dominant north american distance runners. After a 2:18:37 debut marathon last year, he's struggled to find those next 37 seconds, most recently at the McKirdy Micro Marathon. In this interview, Adam talks about his running history, his marathon progression, his shoe rotation, and his goals for CIM 2023 on the last day of the American OTQ window.

Interview date: October 30, 2023 Adam Wood on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@AdamWoodRuns Adam Wood on IG: https://www.instagram.com/adammwoodd/ The Woods Runs on IG: https://www.instagram.com/thewoodsrun/
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko
item.itunes:summary:

item.title: Bonus Episode! Makenna Myler. October 19, 2023
item.description:
Makenna Myler is a pro runner for ASICS, and she just ran a US Olympic Trials Qualifying time at the McKirdy Micro Marathon. In this interview, Makenna gives us a race recap, goes over her extensive ASICS shoe rotation, and talks about her coaching change, moving to Flagstaff, and racing six months postpartum.

item.title: Bonus Episode! Rory Linkletter post World Championships Marathon
item.description:
Rory Linkletter is a pro marathoner for Puma and just ran one of the best races of his life in Budapest at the World Championships Marathon. We sat down to break down the race, reviewed his Puma running shoe rotation in the buildup, and looked ahead to his chase of the Olympic 2:08 standard for Paris 2024.


Follow Rory https://www.instagram.com/rory_linkletter/

item.title: Tina Muir. August 22, 2023
item.description:
This week's guest is Tina Muir, co-author of the book, Becoming a Sustainable Runner. Tina and I first met at TRE last year, and we've become friends ever since. I'm thrilled to be reading her book, as it touches upon a lot of topics that have been slowly nagging at me over the past couple years. It's a timely book, and I hope you enjoy this conversation.


Follow Tina at her podcast https://runningforreal.com/

Buy the book https://becomingasustainablerunner.com/


Note: I'm leaving for World's tomorrow and then I'll be heading to UTMB. This will be the last podcast for this week, and there won't be any podcasts next week.

item.title: Friday July 28, 2023
item.description:
I just came back from three days in Baltimore for the Believe in the Run summer party and then 5 days in Colorado, running in the mountains. There's so much to discuss and so many packages to open.

Also, I'll be gone next week for family vacation, so there won't be any livestreams next week, which got me thinking... I have a lot of travel coming up through the rest of the year. Should we start having guest hosts to take over the show while I'm gone? If so, who should it be?
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko

He is currently spending his summer, running in Flagstaff, and the videos are worth a watch. Subscribe to Parker Max on youtube https://www.youtube.com/@ParkerMax/

item.title: Bonus Episode: Jacob Timm. Tinman Masters
item.description:
Decades ago, Jacob Timm was a D1 athlete who never was able to run as fast as his potential. Now in his 40s, he is chasing records with Tinman Elite as their first professional masters athlete.  


Jacob Timm on IG https://www.instagram.com/jacobtiimm/ 

Tinman Elite on IG https://www.instagram.com/tinmanelite/


Jacob and I reference a near death experience that Jacob suffered. It has been thoroughly covered in an interview with Drew Hunter on the @Tinman YouTube channel. For further context, I highly recommend watching the full interview

https://youtu.be/vzuh2nT1F3U

item.title: Bonus Episode! Dakotah Lindwurm - June 20, 2023
item.description: I had a chance to speak with Dakotah Lindwurm after her 2nd place finish at Grandma's Marathon 2023. As the 2021 and 2022 champion, Dakotah was the favorite for the three-peat and also for a potential course record. We talked about how her day went, how she's processing that day, and what her future looks like as she heads towards a fall marathon and the US Olympic Marathon Trials. Dakotah on Strava https://www.strava.com/pros/26341222 Dakotah on IG https://www.instagram.com/dakotahlindwurm/
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko
item.itunes:summary: I had a chance to speak with Dakotah Lindwurm after her 2nd place finish at Grandma's Marathon 2023. As the 2021 and 2022 champion, Dakotah was the favorite for the three-peat and also for a potential course record. We talked about how her day went, how she's processing that day, and what her future looks like as she heads towards a fall marathon and the US Olympic Marathon Trials. This interview was recorded on Pearl St. in Boulder. June 7, 2023 Sydney on IG https://www.instagram.com/sydney_gidabuday_/?hl=en

item.title: Bonus Episode: Emma Bates!
item.description:
I had the chance to interview Emma Bates in Boulder at an ASICS event. We talked about toxic mold, why her World Championship Marathon PB performance was a disappointment for her, and what she needs to do to get to a 2:18 marathon goal time (which would be an American record).

This episode was pre-recorded and not-live on June 6, 2023, but it was a such a good conversation, I thought I'd put it up here on the Kofuzi Run Club podcast. I don't normally do a lot of athlete interviews. If you like these conversations, let me know by DM or comment when the video version of this conversation goes up on the Kofuzi Run Club YouTube channel.


Follow Emma Bates on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/emmajanelbates/

Follow Team Boss https://www.instagram.com/__teamboss__/

item.title: Ashley Mateo. June 1, 2023
item.description:
This week, I'm bringing on my friend, Ashley Mateo. Ashley is a journalist, writer, and editor. If you've ever read running content in a magazine, you're probably already familiar with her work. She's also someone who has been on some of the same adventures I've been on over the past couple of years, so I'm glad to spend some time with her again on this livestream.


Ashley on The Rundown https://ashleymateo.substack.com Ashley IG https://www.instagram.com/ashleymateo

item.title: Name That Pro Athlete. May 31, 2023
item.description: We played a game where I show you a picture of a pro runner when they're wearing non-running clothes. TBH, this one is going to be real tough to listen to, as I'm terrible at describing how people look, and also, the way that I'm colorblind, Im particularly bad at describing hair color. But, we do have a nice discussion after the game about what to do with future games and future potential guests.

item.title: The Feed Unboxing. April 13, 2023
item.description:
I ordered this so long ago, I forgot what was in it. I hope it's Maurten.


Boston is coming up. Here are some shakeouts we can run together:

Saturday 2pm: Puma shakeout https://www.eventbrite.com/e/594741506857

Saturday 6pm: Livestream Live at ASICS Shop. Join me and special guests, American marathoners Ben True and Nico Montanez for a live taping of the Kofuzi Run Club livestream. 

https://www.eventbrite.com/e/asics-athlete-meetup-tickets-615213047787

Sunday 9am: Rabbit x Kofuzi Run Club https://strava.app.link/tiLRuEpwLyb

Monday after the race: Bandit Portrait Photos https://www.eventbrite.com/e/bandit-x-boston-marathon-post-race-medal-portraits-tickets-602826990757



If you have footage that you'd like to send me, upload clips to me at https://kofuzi.wetransfer.com

Good luck!

item.title: Trivia Tuesday - The Things You Find at GNC
item.description:
You would think that a runner would be able to find a lot of things they might need at a GNC, but you would be wrong. Today's trivia is based on things that I actually found upon even just a cursory inspection of the GNC website. 

My apologies to people who love GNC. I'm sure there's a lot of normal stuff there, but this trivia is not about the normal stuff.

Don't forget, this Saturday at 9am, the Kofuzi Run Club will be meeting at the WWII Memorial for a few miles around the mall to preview the cherry blossoms! See the Kofuzi Run Club group on Strava for details.

item.title: Rory Linkletter
item.description:
In the last few months, Rory Linkletter has run PBs in the marathon, the half marathon (setting a Canadian record), and the 3k. And he’s just getting started. Not only is Rory a great runner, but he’s also heavily engaged in the running community with his socials, his Strava, and a running podcast. Today, we’ll talk about his recent races, being an unsponsored pro who is also a shoe nerd, and what the future holds.

1:12 3k PB

4:20 Rory Doesn’t Like a Taper

6:09 collaborating with Coach Ryan Hall

8:26 Atlanta Publix Half Marathon

12:03 favorite style of race

13:21 Rory vs. Ben

item.dc:creator: Michael Ko
item.itunes:summary:
In the last few months, Rory Linkletter has run PBs in the marathon, the half marathon (setting a Canadian record), and the 3k. And he’s just getting started. Not only is Rory a great runner, but he’s also heavily engaged in the running community with his socials, his Strava, and a running podcast. Today, we’ll talk about his recent races, being an unsponsored pro who is also a shoe nerd, and what the future holds.

item.title: Sydney Gidabuday January 21, 2022
item.description:
Sydney Gidabuday dominated the DII running scene, winning 7 national championships and running the school record for the 5k at powerhouse Adams St. Now, he's back running under a Vigil system with Roots Running and looking to build upon the success of last year's 3rd place finish at the USATF 5k National Championships (13:53). He recently hit a PR of 61:09 at the Houston Half Marathon, and I was lucky enough to sit down with Syd to discuss this race and what he's hoping for in 2022.

Follow Syd on Strava https://www.strava.com/athletes/694472#interval?interval=202201&interval_type=week&chart_type=miles&year_offset=0

Get the Roots Running Merch! https://www.rootsrunning.store/collections/frontpage/products/syd-the-squid-t-shirt?variant=40970509811898

Follow Syd on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/sydney_gidabuday_/

Follow Syd on Twitter https://twitter.com/SydneyGetabooty

item.title: Monday Mukbang! - Fancy Plants Cafe
item.description:
trying some vegan pastries for the Monday Mukbang. 

This will be the last livestream for the week. Tuesday, I'll be traveling to Austin. Wednesday, I'll be at The Running Event all day. Thursday, I'll be with Believe in the Run and Puma for a morning shakeout run at 8am and a podcast (I won't be speaking) at 12:30pm. Friday, I'll be speaking on a panel with Matt Chittim at CIM Expo in Sacramento. Saturday, I'll be doing a shakeout run at 8am in front of the capitol building in Sacramento. Sunday, we race!

Hope I can see you IRL in either Austin or Sacramento this week!

item.title: Sunday December 27, 2020
item.description:
stryd gives a critical power number, which equates to one's power at threshold effort. from there, stryd defines power zones as

easy 65-80% critical power

moderate 80-90% critical power

threshold 90-100% critical power

interval 100-115% critical power

repetition 115-300% critical power
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko
item.itunes:summary:
stryd gives a critical power number, which equates to one's power at threshold effort. from there, stryd defines power zones as

easy 65-80% critical power

moderate 80-90% critical power

threshold 90-100% critical power

interval 100-115% critical power

repetition 115-300% critical power

item.title: Sunday June 14, 2020
item.description: It's been cold, believe it or not, here in Guttenberg, so I'm trying to stay warm while in the basement. It's been hoodie weather for me. For today's livestream, we talked about which shoes stack up best against the Endorphin Pro, Covid news anxiety, and when marathons will come back (and what will that even look like).   Thanks so much for tuning in everybody!

item.title: Happy Hour - Revolution Brewing Citra-Hero
item.description:
Today, I'm drinking Revolution Brewing's Citra Hero, a delicious iteration of their Anti-Hero, made in this version with Citra hops. And, I'm drinking it out of a Jabba Lives Koozie! A pure inside joke for fans of the Kofuzi Run Club livestream, I hope you'll enjoy. You can pick one up at my etsy shop now.

https://www.etsy.com/listing/809127388/jabba-lives-koozie?ref=shop_home_active_1&frs=1&crt=1

item.title: Trivia Tuesday - Iowa Running
item.description:
The topic for today is Iowa Running history, as I am currently spending time in Eastern Iowa. But this was a topic I chose before I checking to see whether I could fill out an entire Trivia Tuesday episode. I had to really dig deep for this one. Thank you for humoring me. 

But I don't think it's so much that there isn't Iowa Running history, I think that perhaps my attempt were thwarted by the fact that any google search involving Iowa and running tends to bring up political campaign news. 

In either event, thanks everyone for tuning in today, even Mario. I love you my brother. 

If you'd like to participate in the livestream, check back daily on youtube at 3pm central.

item.title: Monday Mukbang - Skratch Matcha Green Tea and Lemon
item.description:
Today, I try some Matcha Green Tea and Lemon, a delicious flavor, and we reminisce about Snapple and Mitch Hedberg. And we talked about running shoes and taco pizza.

It was a lot of fun today. Thanks for tuning in. Please join us and participate live, every day on youtube at 3pm Central.

item.title: Happy Hour - Goose Island SPF
item.description:
Thanks for tuning in to the show today. Thursday May 28, 2020. I’ll be drinking Goose Island’s SPF, a low IBU fruit beer with Amarillo hops. Feel free to celebrate the near-end of the week with me and your alcoholic or NA beverage of choice. We had a lot of running news and announcements today. A lot of fun.
Check back every day at 3pm to participate in the livestream. Or if the time doesn’t work for you, I also do a daily livestream at 6am on Instagram.

item.title: Five Bingeable YouTube Series - Kofuzi Run Club 013
item.description:
Today, I’ll start the conversation with five bingeable shows that you guys might want to check out. I don’t really watch TV except for local news and weather, so my picks should hopefully be a bit different than what everyone has been bingeing already. And let me know in the livestream what you’ve been watching! You can find these on my playlist, Top 5 https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLliIt6xk5PhZ48FgMZPovgyryb_cqBCr3
Houston Marathon —2020 —2019 —2018 Salomon TV —Every Single Street —The Trail Camp —Kungsleden Jelle’s Marble Run —track relay —funnel race Kitboga —2019 rewind Simone Giertz —makes useless robots
Bonus: Joseph’s Machines —how to get more work done at dinner —treadmill
Thanks for tuning in to the show today.
Check out YouTube every day at 3pm to participate in the livestream. And if the 3pm central livestream doesn't work for you or your timezone, I also go live daily on IG at 6am

item.itunes:summary: I tried live-streaming from the GoPro (the audio is coming from an external mic), and I ran into some technical difficulties at the end. So, the livestream ends a bit abruptly. My apologies for that. I’ll get things figured out eventually.

item.title: Kofuzi Run Club 006
item.description: Audio from the April 22, 2020 YouTube livestream. Sorry the audio isn’t as good this time. The external mic I use for capturing the audio didn’t turn on and so you’ll be listening to iPhone ambient mic. I’ll get it figured out at some point.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ko

item.title: Kofuzi Run Club 005
item.description: This is the audio portion of the afternoon YouTube livestream from April 21, 2020. This was recorded in front of a live virtual audience. Check in daily on YouTube to participate.

item.title: Kofuzi Run Club 004
item.description: Monday, April 20, 2020. This is the audio from a daily YouTube livestream that was recorded in front of a live virtual audience. I hope you enjoy the recording, and please check out the YouTube livestream at 3pm central if you want to participate.

item.title: Kofuzi Run Club 003
item.description: I’ve decided it may now be time to put the live-streams into a more convenient package. The afternoon live-streams can now be found on YouTube. But in case you want to listen only, like while running, this is the audio from the afternoon live-streams. This is a work in progress.
